4
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Of all the potential traffic sources online, there is none bigger than search engines.
The estimated search numbers are staggering…
In fact, of all traffic that is generated online, 93% of it comes from search engines.
Best of all, the vast majority of search clicks are from organic posts. This means that
if you learn search engine optimization (SEO) there are certainly customers out
there looking for what you have to sell.
As you likely know, when it comes to search engines,
Google is the 800-pound gorilla. They own an
estimated 85% of the market. For that reason, we’ll
usually refer to Google when discussing search
engines as a whole.
INTRODUCTION
50,000
Searches a Second
5 Billion
Searches a Day
2 Trillion
Searches a Year
Google
Microsoft Bing
Baidu, Yandex, and DuckDuckGo
Yahoo!
It is worth noting that the only search engine gaining any sort of
market share is DuckDuckGo. They have positioned themselves as the
“anti-evil” search engine where they keep user’s data private.
SEO should be thought of as an investment. Time and money
they invested today may not pay dividends immediately, but
done right, can help you cashflow for many years.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 33 --

5
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
So how do you rank at the top of search engines? That is the million-dollar
question, and one that we’ll tackle in great depth in this manual. While there are
many facets involved in SEO, it is important to understand the basic premise...
Never forget the simple fact that the best result will win in the end. Have patience
and don't take shortcuts. If you follow these best practices and give Google what it
wants, you will win.
Google's job is to give their users the best possible results (period).
Top result on Google for “top sales guru” showing a snippet view
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

6
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Example of the first SERP for the term “Grant Cardone”
SEO BASICS
RESULTS & ALGORITHMS
Before we dig into the “how” of SEO, let’s get clear on some basic fundamentals.
We’ll assume you’ve used search engines, and focus on what you need to
understand as a marketer or business owner.
When a user conducts a search, they are shown what is referred to as a Search
Engine Results Pages, or SERPs for short. Generally speaking, there can be millions
of results for a search, but only 10 primary results are displayed per page.
As you’ve seen, users have the capability to view more SERPs by clicking the
“pagination” menu at the bottom of the screen. That said, unless researching a
topic deeply, few users go deeper into the results. This makes the top results
extremely valuable.
Search Engine Results Pages
FACT: 75% of users don’t even scroll down on the first page.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 33 --

7
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
I like to say, “If you're not first, you’re last”. With search
engines, the first spot is where all the money is. If you don’t
think you can get in the top 3 results, it isn’t worth bothering.
Let’s dig a little deeper into the actual components of a result. You’ve seen these
many times in your own search usage, but it is important to understand how each
aspect correlates to your website. Here is a basic organic result…
These standard elements are controlled on your website. We will discuss them each
in more detail, as these are the primary “on page” elements of search engine
optimization.
As you’ve likely seen, an organic result can have many types of variations, which are
referred to as “rich snippets”. Rich snippets are appended to the standard search
result, and will depend on the search term queried and content of the page.
Needless to say, it is advantageous to have this type of snippets added on to results,
as they make them more noticeable and clickable.
Here is an example of a result displayed for the search “grant cardone events”...
Anatomy of Search Result
Page Title - The clickable part of the result
Meta Description - The description shown below
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 33 --

8
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Here are some of the additional snippet types that may be attached to a result…
Techie Note
Google uses a standard protocol to pull data from your website to use in these
features snippets. Good news for non-techies is that achieving placement of snippets
in results can be easily done with common website builders such as Wordpress,
Shopify or Wix. This is usually done either automatically or with the use of a plugin.
Sitelinks
Ratings
Maps
Products
Image
Video
Cards
Numbered lists
Basic info
Local map results are very common, as nearly half searches are localized. Reviews
are absolutely essential for local businesses to rank. Pay close attention to your
Google business profile and consider using a service such as Nicejob to increase
ranking on the map.
In addition to the primary results, there are also other result types that will appear
on a SERP based on the term that has been searched. Some terms will return none
of these various result types, while others may return multiple.
While there are many types of results that Google includes in their primary results,
Google most commonly displays results which they have search options for.
Featured Result Types
Google commonly displays these search types in “All” results
Local Results
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 33 --

9
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Google will link to videos by displaying thumbnails whenever they consider them
relevant to the search. These results are most often from YouTube, so always take
SEO factors into consideration when uploading your videos.
Video Results
Google will include image results on the main SERP for terms when they will
provide value. Bottom line is to add original images whenever possible, and ensure
they are optimized. We’ll discuss strategy for optimizing images in more detail.
Image Results
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 33 --

10
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
For certain terms Google will favor recent events over dated content. Terms such
as this are difficult to rank for unless you are continually publishing articles and
press releases. We’ll discuss overall content strategy in more detail.
While you have less control over the type of result that Google displays, you should
be aware of which type of results your desired terms are returning. It is
recommended that you always search terms for yourself. For example, the SERP
for a “how to” term will consistently produce video results as well as “people also
asked” dropdowns.
News Results
Products
Flights
Hotels
Jobs
Events
Podcasts
Recipes
Sports
Stocks
While the SEO gurus will recommend using fancy tools, the
most powerful tool you can use when optimizing is Google
itself. Type terms in and see what the “Almighty G” displays.
If you’re trying to understand the technical aspects of the
algorithm, you’re wasting your time. I didn’t win with search
because I was smart. I won because I took massive action.
Now that we’ve clarified what Google and the search engines deliver to users, let’s
discuss how they decide which pages to rank.
Google is continuously “crawling” all of the world’s websites using
their “spiders”. These spiders pull information into its vast data
network. When a user submits a search query, Google uses its
proprietary “algorithm” to determine which results to display. The
easiest way to think of an algorithm is an equation that takes
many factors into consideration, many of which we will discuss.
There are many other types of results that are industry specific…
Search Algorithm
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 33 --

11
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
While Google does not publish the exact formula it uses to power its algorithm,
there are certainly agreed upon ranking factors and best practices. While we will
cover these reliable SEO principles in this manual, be aware that Google is
continually updating its algorithm. In the past, you may have heard these updates
referred to by names like Penguin and Panda.
If you want to dig deeper into what drives the Google
algorithm, it is recommended that you follow Google’s
official SEO blog. It is full of valuable insights and is the
best way to keep up with ongoing updates.
10xresource.com/seo11
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

12
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
KEYWORD RESEARCH
If Google’s algorithm is the machine that drives results, keywords are the fuel that
makes it work. Generally speaking, ranking a website doesn’t happen by accident.
You must first decide which terms you wish to rank for, then enact your plan.
While there are certainly more advanced tools, to get started researching you can
use Google’s free keyword planner. The easy-to-use tool, which is located inside of
Google Ads, provides basic keyword data and helps you brainstorm keyword
phrases. 10xresource.com/seo10
Google’s free keyword planner is great for the basics.
While you may wish to rank for one-word phrases, keywords are generally in multiple
word phrases. As a rule, the shorter and more general the phrase, the more searches it
has and the more difficult it is to rank for.
We recommend thinking of your keyword phrases modularly…
This is the main term included in your desired phase.
You will not generally rank for this term without
significant investment of time and effort.
Keyword Strategy
Root Keyword Phrase
Example: “real estate”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 33 --

13
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Ultimately, you will choose one primary keyword phrase for every page on your website.
You will then modify that keyword phrase so that you are also targeting a longtail term in
the process.
Ranking for the big terms can take years, especially for a
new website. Be sure to include longtail phrases so you
can get immediate results while you play the long game.
This is the main phrase you will attempt to rank for.
It should contain your root keyword phrase, and
have additional context to make it more defined.
Primary Keyword Phase
Example: “real estate ira”
This is a variation of your primary phrase that adds
even more detail. The search volume of these is
lower, making the competition much lower.
Longtail Keyword Phrases
Example: “real estate roth ira”
Example results displayed by google’s free keyword tool

14
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
While every SEO expert will use unique strategies and terminology, there are only three
main factors that you should consider on a high level…
Simply put, this is how many people are searching for a particular term. This is
generally shown on a monthly basis, keep in mind that some phrases are seasonal.
This is the case for many ecommerce phrases which spike during the holiday season.
Remember that it isn’t all about how many searches, it’s about
how many clicks you get. Unless you’ve got a big budget and
dedicated team, the most searched terms are difficult.
Keywords are not affected by capitalization
Words like “of” and “and” are not significant
Emojis and punctuation will break a phrase
Google will associate similar terms (variations)
Unique phrases are still able to be ranked for
Always start by doing a basic Google search
Keyword Selection
Search Volume	1
Here are a few additional things to keep in mind with keywords…
While expensive tools give you fancy scores to evaluate
competition, use your common sense. You aren’t going to
beat Wikipedia or Amazon, no matter how hard you try.
This is a more qualitative factor, unlike search volume which is quantitative. It is
important to take into consideration the quality of the pages ranking at the top
for the term. The number of competitors is less important, since you are only
concerned with the first page.
Competition	2
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 33 --

15
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
The real value of ranking for a keyword is often overlooked.
The bottom line is if you rank #1 for a term that doesn’t turn
into leads and sales, it doesn’t matter as much.
Before selecting a keyword term, you’ve got to take into account the likelihood
that ranking for that phrase will turn into actual business. To do this, you need to
consider the intent of the searcher. Intent is an important concept to always
consider in SEO.
Since keyword research is so critical to success, we recommend you make it an
immediate priority. Here are some basic steps…
Keyword Value	3
Buyer Intent Example:
“play board game” is searched 1000 times per month
“buy board game” is searched 720 times per month
If you were selling board games, which one would you rather rank for?
Keyword Planning
Google’s keyword ideas for “sales training” (notice #1!)
Create a spreadsheet and list out
every possible idea. Start by using
root keywords and wide topics,
then add more ideas from there.
Any keyword tool you use will
help with ideas.
STEP 1 	Brainstorm master list of topics for root keywords
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 33 --

16
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
The search volume is something that is provided by Google’s free tool. Keep in mind
that the competition column Google displays is shown for advertisers, not organic
results. You can also get some indication as to the value of a term based on what
advertisers pay, but the true value for your business will always be unique.
To get more detailed analysis you’ll want to use a paid tool such as Moz, which is
described below.
STEP 2 	Consider search volume, competition, and value
Remember that you don’t need every page to rank, just the
right ones. Cardone Capital had one blog page lead to millions
in investment, while most pages amounted to nothing.
Create a list of all the main pages on your website and decide on a primary keyword
phrase for each page. It is important to not try to rank for more than one primary
phrase per page. Keep in mind that you will also be creating content, usually in the
form of blog posts, to rank for many of the phrases on your list.
STEP 3 	Choose the primary keyword phrase for each page
Once you’ve chosen a primary phrase, add some detail to make the phrase longer
and more specific. This is done by adding a niche or making the topic more
specific. It is important to make sure that your longtail phrase still contains your
primary phrase.
STEP 4 	Expand that primary phrase into a longtail phase
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 33 --

17
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Since Google’s algorithm is smart enough to recognize similar phrases, it is often
good to include a few variations of your primary keyword. This can be done by
reordering or using synonyms. For example, variations of “real estate ira” could
include “IRA for real estate” or “properties IRA”.
While you can do basic keyword research using Google, if you
want to dig deeper you need a professional tool. Moz is a great
option for keyword research and all of your SEO needs. While
they offer sophisticated paid tools, you can try the basic
functions for free 10xresource.com/seo7
STEP 5 	Identify several additional keyword phrase variations
Primary Keyword Phrase: 	Example Longtail Phrases:
“chocolate cake recipe” 	“chocolate cake recipe for beginners”
“keto chocolate cake recipe”
“homemade chocolate cake recipe”
“inexpensive chocolate cake recipe”

OPTIMIZATION FACTORS
Now that we understand the essence of how search works, let’s get into the specifics
of increasing your rank. For the sake of simplification, we are going to break down the
major ranking factors into 3 major groups…
Performance	1
Content and Metadata	2
Incoming Links	3
Techie Note
Remember that websites aren’t ranked, individual pages are. With that said, your
website as a whole does certainly play an important role. Your site domain will
develop what is referred to as an “authority score”. While this is an unofficial
measurement, most SEO software such as Moz or SEMrush provide their own
proprietary authority score.
Performance	1
The last thing that Google wants to do is send their users to websites
that fail to deliver. Here is what to focus on to make sure your site’s
performance is world-class…
People all want to know which factor is most important, and
the answer is all of them. While you need a good site, in the
end it comes down to creating content and massive buzz.
Let’s break down each of the three major factors in more detail…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

19
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Our website is slowed down on desktop by lots of big images.
If you have an old-fashioned, clunky website or a rudimentary sales funnel with
only one page, you might be wasting time and energy trying to optimize it. Get
started by doing a quick speed check to see where you stand.
Page Speed	A
Test your website speed at 10xresource.com/seo32
Things that can
SLOW DOWN your site…
Things that can
SPEED UP your site…
It is essential that your website is laid out in a way that allows Google’s spiders to
easily crawl all of our pages. This is done by creating a well designed menu and
making sure your pages are linked in a strategic manner.
Site Crawlability	B
Large images
Fancy functionality
Complicated menu
Too many plugins
Lousy hosting
Compressed images
Simple, clean code
Easy to navigate
Only essential plugins
Hosting on CDN

-- 19 of 33 --

20
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Everyone wants to discover the “secret” to SEO. The secret
is that there is no secret. Give users a great experience,
and Google will want to send their users your way.
Remember that Google’s goal is to display their users the best possible results.
One of the ways they determine this is by how long a user stays on your site
after clicking a search result. Make your experience “sticky” by making sure
people find what they want.
Page Stickiness	C
Search engine spiders read text, not images or videos.
They also only follow links that are in the form of text.
Site Maps
One of the essential aspects of any well optimized site is a site map.
This is essentially a list of all your pages that allows your site to be
crawled more easily. Pages can also be weighted to tell Google
which ones are most important. Site Maps are easy to create in a
content management system (CMS) such as Wordpress.
One of the most important website metrics to track is bounce rate.
Bounce rate is the percentage of people who leave within seconds of
hitting your page. This can be tracked by installing Google Analytics
One of the most overlooked aspects of site performance is assuring that it works
for all users. Accessibility is about making sure anyone, regardless of their
handicap or challenges, can achieve their desired result. There are several tools,
including Accessibee, that allow you to do this relatively easily.
Site Accessibility	D
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

21
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
People act like SEO is so different now, but it’s barely
changed in 20 years. Tricks don’t work. Content is king, and
if you give Google what they want, they will reward you.
Now that your site is performing well and easy to crawl, it is time to focus
on what the spiders will find when crawling your pages. Here are the
essential elements…
This refers to the actual words on a page that can be read by users. Search
engines read text in much the same way. While we will discuss content creation
in more detail, some essential aspects include…
Page Text	A
Metadata is the term used to describe all of the information that Google reads
from within your HTML code that is not readable by humans. We will go into
great detail in later chapters about how this is done, but the primary metadata
fields are…
Metadata	B
Content and Metadata	2
Number of Words
Keyword Frequency
Keyword Positioning
Outbound Links
Page Title 	Meta Description 	Meta Keywords
Don’t stress when you hear technical SEO jargon. These
days systems like Wordpress and Shopify make it easy to
optimize your pages, and you don’t even need a techie.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 33 --

22
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Google spiders try to mimic human behavior. For example, text
that is bold is more important than text that is not. Info at the
top of the page is more important than info at the bottom.
Bolded font
Italicized font
Underlined font
H1 Header Tag
H2, H3, etc
Image Alt Text
Somewhere in between metadata and simple page text is how your text is
formatted. In other words, not all words on your pages carry the same weight.
Most website CMS systems allow you to easily edit formatting in multiple ways…
Text Emphasis	C
Incoming Links	3
Links are the single biggest way to boost your search engine rank.
Every link your page receives is like getting a virtual vote in the eyes of
Google. We will cover strategies for getting these links in a later
chapter, but here are the basics…
An analogy often used to describe the concept of links is "link juice."
Imagine that every time a page links to another it is passing along valuable
juice, increasing that page’s rank. It is important to spread your link juice
around wisely!
Internal links - Links from one of your pages to another
Inbound links - Links to your site from someone else’s
Outbound links - Links leaving your site to someone else’s
Here is a little more content to understand links and their value…
Link Quantity - How many links, both internal and external, are
pointing to the webpage.
Link Quality - There are a number of factors that determine the
quality of a link a page receives.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 33 --

23
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
SEO used to be about getting tons of links from rinky dink
websites. Nowadays it is better to get one link from a
world-class media source than a thousand from small ones.
The more similar the linking page is to your page the better.
Relevancy	A
The bigger and more reputable the linking site, the more the impact.
Authority	B
The text in the link tells Google what that page should rank for.
If you were trying to rank for the term “Las Vegas carwash”…
Anchor Text	C
Makeup Blog 	Basketball Site 	Small Boost
Bob’s Blog 	Small Boost
BoB’s
Blog
Huge Boost
Basketball Site	Sports Blog 	Huge Boost
Check it out now 	Small Boost
Huge Boost	Best carwash in Las Vegas

CREATION PHASE
Recommended Resource
It can be difficult to understand linking strategy
conceptually, so we have provided several flowcharts for
visual examples. Keep in mind, linking is as much an art as
it is a science. There is no right or wrong way to do it. These
documents are accompanied by an explainer video to
make them easier to grasp.
Onpage Linking: 10xdownload.com/seo2 	Offpage Linking: 10xdownload.com/seo3
To understand SEO from another perspective, it is often broken into two separate
categories. These categories are distinguished by where the optimization activity
takes place…
Onpage Optimization
Everything on/in your website
that affects your search rank
Offpage Optimization
Everyone outside your website
that affects your search rank
Off Page Link
Building Ideas

Podcast Pages
Video Pages
Articles Sites
Online Forums
Local Organizations
National Directories
Niche Directories
Guest Blogging
Press Releases
Media Stories

25
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
A link from a website with high domain authority is
always trusted by both robots and people. Having a
backlink from a .edu site is the holy grail!
A.I. 	+ 	S.E.O. 	=
Contrary to popular belief, Google has no problem with A.I. generated content.
They’ve publicly stated that they do not penalize content simply because it is
generated by artificial intelligence. All they care about is giving their users the
best experience, so as long as you’re producing quality materials, you can
certainly use A.I. to save you time and energy!
What Google Looks For In Content
Experience 	Expertise 	Authoritativeness 	Trustworthiness

28
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
SEO TERMINOLOGY
Pages that redirect users to a different page on a site. 301’s are put in place when a web
page is deleted or does not exist.
301 REDIRECT
This error occurs when a user tries to access a page that does not or no longer exists.
404 NOT FOUND
It is a sequence of actions performed, including calculations and automated reasoning,
that determine the ranking of a page.
ALGORITHM
An element for describing images to search engines.
ALT TEXT
A tool for computing and analyzing a set of data or statistics. We use analytical statistics
like website traffic, users, revenue, traffic sources, site goals, and the behavior pattern of
users to compare the progress of an SEO campaign.
ANALYTICS
Text within a website that is clickable and links to another page on the site or a different
domain.
ANCHOR TEXT
A value that is often given by SEO tools to describe the significance of a particular website
or domain.
AUTHORITY SCORE
An aggressive strategy that does not adhere to search engine policy and guidelines. This
strategy can result in a penalty to a site from search engines, preventing it from ranking
organically.
BLACK HAT SEO
A dead hyperlink that directs to a non existent link.
BROKEN LINK
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 33 --

29
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
The rate that users click on a website from organic or paid search results. This is a way to
gauge title and description performance.
CLICK THROUGH RATE (CTR)
The sites that are currently in the highest positions that would need to be beaten to
achieve the desired rank.
COMPETITION
CMS is an application, such as Wordpress, used to manage digital content as an
individual or with a team.
CONTENT MANAGEMENT SYSTEM (CMS)
Process of using a third-party app to search a website and collect data including pages,
status codes, and SEO elements such as titles and meta descriptions.
CRAWL
Any variety of website that lists other sites and provides links to them.
DIRECTORIES
A chunk of content that is present on more than multiple pages or website on the
internet.
DUPLICATE CONTENT
A type of SEO campaign tactic used to increase rankings specifically for an eCommerce
brand, category page or product pages.
ECOMMERCE SEO
This is an application that collects and reports on web traffic, allowing marketers to track
progress of conversions, revenue, traffic sources, and paid campaigns to a website.
GOOGLE ANALYTICS
The listing that Google creates for a business that can be claimed and managed by a
business owner.
GOOGLE BUSINESS PROFILE
A free online service to monitor a website’s index and crawl status.
GOOGLE SEARCH CONSOLE
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 33 --

30
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Also known as a header tags, this is typically the largest text on the page and is the main
idea of the page.
H1 TAG
Short for Hypertext Markup Language. This is the system for creating and marking up
websites with content, graphics, links, and images.
HTML
Links from other sources that direct users to your website or landing page. Inbound links
are also known as a backlink.
INBOUND LINK
A hyperlink of text or an image that links to another page on the same site. This can be
used to incorporate relevant keyword driven anchor text.
INTERNAL LINK
A research on competitors and a site’s current rankings to help develop a list of keywords
to target for your onpage optimization, content development, and link building.
KEYWORD RESEARCH
A black hat SEO tactic for adding a big amount of keywords onto a page or website to
trick organic search engines.
KEYWORD STUFFING
Alternative versions of keyword phrases that have the same meaning, but are expressed
with different words.
KEYWORD VARIATIONS
A landing page targets a specific keyword and mostly has a call to action for users to
perform, like purchase or subscribe.
LANDING PAGE
The process of getting links from other websites to improve a site's authority.
LINK BUILDING
Any keyword that is longer in length and very specific, generally receiving less search
volume
LONGTAIL KEYWORD
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 33 --

31
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
An HTML markup that allows users to see a short description of a web page. It's usually
160 characters in length.
META DESCRIPTION
One of the less important onpage optimization factors that allows a website owner to
notify Google of the desired terms to rank
META KEYWORDS
HTML tag on a link that tells search engines to ignore the link.
NOFOLLOW
All factors of search engine optimization that happen outside of the website being
optimized for, primarily backlinks from other sites.
OFFPAGE SEO
All factors of search engine optimization that happen outside of the website being
optimized for, primary the perfomance, code, and content of the individual webpages.
ONPAGE SEO
Free listing results in a search engine that appears because it's relevant to someone's
keyword search.
ORGANIC RESULTS
Links within a website that directs users to another site.
OUTBOUND LINK
A value assigned to a page as a measure of popularity or importance. This scale is 0-10
with 10 being of the highest authority.
PAGERANK
Content that is well written for users and not just for SEO.
QUALITY CONTENT
A general term used to describe how similar the topic of one webpage is to another.
RELEVANCY
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 33 --

32
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Refers to the content displayed within a SERP. In addition to standard results, Google also
displays results such as images, videos, news, products, and more.
RESULT TYPE
Specific "add-ons" that Google will add to particular results, such as reviews or site links.
RICH SNIPPET
A text file on a website that tells search engines to ignore some parts of the site.
ROBOTS.TXT
A program that searches millions of pages and documents that helps people find the
information they are looking for in the internet using keywords or phrases.
SEARCH ENGINE
A keyword or phrase that a users type into a search engine to find related websites and
information.
SEARCH QUERY
The quantity of searches for any given search term.
SEARCH VOLUME
Search Engine Marketing is digital marketing across organic and paid advertising
platforms to help companies' content rank higher among search engine traffic.
SEM
Search Engine Optimization is the digital marketing practice for increasing free traffic
and visibility to a website or web page through search engine rankings.
SEO
Search Engine Results Page displayed by a search engine based on the keyword typed in
by a user. Typically, the title and meta description are shown to the user, giving them
relevant information about the website before clicking.
SERP
A file where you can see about the website's content and the relationship between them.
SITE MAP
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 33 --

33
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
A web crawler is a tool that discovers and indexes new and updated pages on a site.
SPIDER
HTML code on a web page the represent the most relevant keyword and information
about the page. It is important to add and optimize this as this play an essential part in
terms ot organic ranking.
TITLE TAG
Uniform Resource Locator is a web address that points to a unique page on a website,
FTP, email, or database access.
URL
Usability is defined as the ease at which an average person can use the software or
website to achieve specific goals. Usability is comprised of learnability, memorability,
efficiency, satisfaction, and errors.
USABILITY
SEO techniques that are aligned with search engine best practices and are user focused
with the goal of improving the organic visibility of a website.
WHITE HAT SEO
The expected goal or mindset of a user who is performing a search.
USER INTENT
